asset_type: contact sheet for academic paper framework SVG icon refinement
prompt:
Create a clean contact sheet of 25 minimalist academic vector-style icons on a white background. Use one icon per grid cell with generous whitespace around every icon, no text, no labels, no watermark. Style: consistent thin dark outline, subtle pale blue fills, rounded corners, publication-quality PPT-friendly symbols, readable at small size. Optimize each icon by its meaning, not by copying any source-paper figure. Icons in order:
1. projection head (projection head, embedding head)
2. loss supervision head (loss head, supervision signal)
3. prompt chain (prompt chain, instruction flow)
4. tool-call block (tool call, function call)
5. planner-executor split (planner executor, planning acting)
6. memory update loop (memory update, episodic memory)
7. evaluator/judge loop (evaluator, judge, critic)
8. debate/committee workflow (debate, committee)
9. chunk-index retrieval (chunk index, document chunks)
10. retriever-reranker chain (reranker, retrieve rerank)
11. context window assembly (context window, context packing)
12. image-text fusion (image text fusion, vision language)
13. audio-text fusion (audio text fusion, speech language)
14. modality gating (modality gate, fusion gate)
15. cross-attention bridge (cross attention bridge, modality bridge)
16. GNN message passing (message passing, graph neural network)
17. knowledge graph (knowledge graph, entity relation)
18. causal graph (causal graph, intervention graph)
19. hypergraph (hypergraph, higher-order graph)
20. molecule graph (molecule graph, chemical graph)
21. neural field representation (neural field, implicit field)
22. NeRF ray field (NeRF rays, camera rays)
23. Gaussian splatting (Gaussian splatting, 3DGS)
24. BEV perception (BEV, bird eye view)
25. point-voxel fusion (point voxel fusion, 3D fusion)
negative_prompt: No source-paper labels, no equations, no metrics, no datasets, no dense UI screenshots, no raster texture, no 3D render shadows, no tiny unreadable details.
intended_postprocess: cut each grid cell, inspect semantics, then programmatically redraw as editable SVG primitives
